project.name: SHL Assessment Recommendation System
project.goal: Build a production-grade AI system that takes a job description or natural language query and recommends the most relevant SHL assessments (max 10) from their product catalog.
project.context: This is a take-home assignment for an AI/GenAI Intern role at SHL (a global leader in talent assessment). The solution will be evaluated on accuracy (MAP@3, Recall@3), code quality, architecture, documentation, and live deployment.
submission_requirements: Live deployed web app URL (Streamlit or equivalent)
GitHub repository with clean code and README

data.description: Pre-scraped SHL product catalog with ~300+ assessments. Already exists in the project root.

schema_per_item.name: string — assessment name (e.g., 'Core Java (New)', 'OPQ32r')
schema_per_item.link: string — URL to SHL product page
schema_per_item.description: string — detailed description of what the assessment measures
schema_per_item.keys: string[] — test type categories (e.g., ['Knowledge & Skills'], ['Personality & Behavior'], ['Ability & Aptitude'])
schema_per_item.duration: string — e.g., '30 minutes' or '' if unknown
schema_per_item.duration_raw: string — raw scraped duration text, needs parsing

preprocessing_instructions: STEP 1: Load the JSON file and filter to items where status == 'ok'
STEP 2: Parse duration — extract integer minutes from 'duration' field. If empty, try 'duration_raw'. If both empty, set to null.
STEP 3: Build a 'document' string per assessment by concatenating: name + description + keys + job_levels + duration + remote + adaptive. This is what gets embedded.

STEP 4: The concatenated document format should be: 'Assessment: {name}. Description: {description}. Test Types: {keys joined}. Job Levels: {job_levels joined}. Duration: {duration}. Remote: {remote}. Adaptive: {adaptive}.'
STEP 5: Store cleaned data as a list of dicts with all original fields PLUS the new 'document' string and 'duration_minutes' int field.
data.reasoning: Concatenating all fields into the document string is critical because semantic embedding models need full context. If you only embed the name, a query like 'assess data analysis skills' won't match 'Numerical Reasoning' — but the description mentions data interpretation, which WILL match.

architecture.diagram: User Query → [Stage 1: LLM Feature Extraction] → [Stage 2: Sentence-BERT + FAISS Semantic Search] → [Stage 3: LLM Re-ranking + Hard Filtering] → Top 10 JSON Response

stage_1_feature_extraction.what: Use Gemini to parse the input query/JD and extract structured requirements
stage_1_feature_extraction.why: Raw JDs are messy. Extracting structured features lets us apply hard filters AFTER semantic search, which dramatically improves relevance.
stage_1_feature_extraction.fallback: If Gemini API fails (rate limit, network), skip this stage and go directly to Stage 2 with the raw query. The system must NEVER crash due to LLM unavailability.

stage_2_semantic_search.what: Encode query with Sentence-BERT, search FAISS index for top-20 similar assessments
stage_2_semantic_search.index: FAISS IndexFlatIP (inner product, since embeddings are normalized = cosine similarity)
stage_2_semantic_search.why: Sentence-BERT captures semantic meaning. 'Java programming' matches 'Core Java' even without keyword overlap. FAISS makes this O(1) fast even with 10K+ assessments.
stage_2_semantic_search.reasoning: We retrieve 20 candidates (not 10) because Stage 3 will filter some out based on hard constraints. Over-fetching ensures we still have 10 after filtering.

stage_3_rerank_and_filter.what: Apply hard filters from Stage 1 (duration, remote, job_level), then optionally use Gemini to re-rank remaining candidates
hard_filters: If max_duration_minutes extracted → remove assessments exceeding it
If requires_remote → remove assessments where remote != 'yes'
If job_level extracted → boost assessments matching that level (don't hard-exclude)
stage_3_rerank_and_filter.llm_rerank: Send remaining candidates + original query to Gemini asking: 'Rank these assessments by relevance to this job description. Return ranked list with brief reasoning.'
stage_3_rerank_and_filter.fallback: If Gemini fails, return FAISS results sorted by cosine similarity score (Stage 2 output). This is the baseline fallback.
stage_3_rerank_and_filter.final_output: Top 10 assessments with all required fields

backend_api.why: Async support, automatic Swagger/OpenAPI docs, Pydantic validation, industry standard for ML APIs

embeddings.why: 384-dim embeddings, fast inference, excellent semantic quality, no API key needed, runs locally

vector_search.why: Facebook's battle-tested vector search. In-memory, blazing fast. No external service needed.

llm.why: Free tier (15 RPM), good reasoning, fast. Used for feature extraction and re-ranking only — NOT for core retrieval.
llm.api_key_handling: Load from .env file using python-dotenv. NEVER hardcode.

frontend.why: Ship beautiful UI in 50 lines. SHL themselves use Streamlit internally for AI demos. Fast iteration.

deployment.note: Render free tier sleeps after 15 min inactivity. Add cron ping to /health endpoint.

project_structure.reasoning: Modular structure is critical. Single-file apps get rejected. Separate concerns: data, engine, API, UI.

src/.config.py: Load env vars, model paths, constants
src/.data_loader.py: Load JSON, clean, preprocess, build document strings
src/.llm_client.py: Gemini API wrapper for feature extraction and re-ranking. Includes retry logic and fallback.
src/.recommender.py: Main orchestrator — ties Stage 1 + 2 + 3 together. Single entry point: recommend(query) → list[Assessment]
src/.models.py: Pydantic models for API request/response schemas

api/.main.py: FastAPI app with /health and /recommend endpoints. CORS middleware. Startup event loads FAISS index.

ui/.app.py: Streamlit frontend. Text input, results display as cards, optional filter sidebar.

evaluation/.test_queries.json: 10-20 test queries with expected assessment names
evaluation/.evaluate.py: Compute MAP@3 and Recall@3 against test queries. Print results table.

scripts/.build_index.py: One-time script: load catalog → embed all docs → save FAISS index + metadata to disk
scripts/.preprocess.py: One-time script: clean raw JSON → produce cleaned data file

data/.embeddings.npy: Numpy array of all assessment embeddings

POST /recommend.description: Get assessment recommendations for a query or job description

request_body.query: string — natural language query or full job description (required)

response.query: string — the input query echoed back

recommendations.name: string — assessment name
recommendations.url: string — link to SHL product page

test_queries.query: I am hiring for Java developers who can also handle backend architecture
Need cognitive ability screening for graduate hires, must be remote
Assess leadership and management competencies for director-level role
Data analyst position requiring SQL, Python, and analytical thinking
Quick customer service assessment, under 20 minutes, remote
Full-stack developer assessment covering React and Node.js
Personality and behavioral assessment for general population
Cloud computing skills test for AWS and Azure

evaluation.instructions: Run evaluate.py after building the system. Report MAP@3 and Recall@3 in README. Scores of 0.4-0.7 are acceptable — the point is showing you measured.

implementation_order.reasoning: Build bottom-up: data first, then engine, then API, then UI, then deploy. Each phase should be independently testable.

phases.name: Baseline (DO THIS FIRST)
steps: Run scripts/preprocess.py — load shl_product_catalog.json, clean durations, build document strings, save cleaned_catalog.json
Implement a simple TF-IDF cosine similarity baseline in src/recommender.py
Test with 3-4 manual queries to verify sensible results
This baseline is your FALLBACK if embeddings/LLM break
phases.why: Never start with the complex approach. A working baseline proves the data pipeline works and gives you confidence.

phases.name: Embedding Engine
steps: Implement src/embedder.py — load Sentence-BERT model, encode all documents, build FAISS index
Run scripts/build_index.py — generate and save faiss_index.bin + embeddings.npy
Implement search function: query string → top-K results with scores
Test: verify 'Java developer' returns Java-related assessments

phases.name: LLM Integration
steps: Implement src/llm_client.py — Gemini wrapper with retry logic
Feature extraction prompt: given a JD, extract skills/level/duration/remote as JSON
Re-ranking prompt: given candidates + query, return ranked list
CRITICAL: implement fallback — if Gemini fails, return FAISS-only results
Test both success and failure (disconnect network to test fallback)

phases.name: API Layer
steps: Implement api/main.py — FastAPI app
On startup: load FAISS index + cleaned data into memory
POST /recommend — call recommender.recommend(query), return JSON
GET /health — return {status: healthy}
Add CORS middleware (allow all origins for demo)
Test with curl or httpie

phases.name: Frontend
steps: Implement ui/app.py — Streamlit app
Text area for query input
Call FastAPI /recommend endpoint (or import recommender directly)
Display results as styled cards showing: name, duration, test_type, remote, adaptive, link
Add a sidebar with optional filters (duration slider, remote checkbox)
Add loading spinner during processing

phases.name: Evaluation
steps: Create evaluation/test_queries.json with 8-10 queries
Implement evaluation/evaluate.py — compute MAP@3 and Recall@3
Run evaluation, record scores
Add scores to README

phases.name: Deploy + Document
steps: Write Dockerfile for API
Deploy API to Render.com (connect GitHub repo)
Deploy Streamlit to Streamlit Cloud (connect GitHub repo)
Write README.md with: overview, architecture diagram (Mermaid), setup instructions, API docs, evaluation results, tech stack rationale
Test both deployed URLs work
Set up cron-job.org to ping /health every 10 min (keeps Render awake)

key_prompts.feature_extraction_prompt:
You are an HR assessment expert. Given the following job description, extract these fields as JSON:
- required_skills: list of specific skills mentioned
- job_level: one of [Entry-Level, Graduate, Mid-Professional, Manager, Director, Executive]
- max_duration_minutes: maximum acceptable test duration in minutes (null if not specified)
- requires_remote: true if remote/online testing is needed
- test_type_preference: one of [Knowledge & Skills, Personality & Behavior, Ability & Aptitude, Competencies, Simulations] or null

Job Description:
{query}

Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown.

critical_rules: NEVER hardcode API keys. Always use .env + python-dotenv.
ALWAYS implement fallback when LLM is unavailable. FAISS-only results must still work.
The system must return 1-10 assessments. Never 0, never more than 10.
Response format must match the API contract exactly — name, url, duration, test_type, remote_testing, adaptive_irt.
Pre-compute embeddings at build time (scripts/build_index.py), NOT at request time. Startup loads from disk.
Use Pydantic models for ALL request/response validation.
Add CORS middleware to FastAPI (allow_origins=['*'] for demo).
Streamlit can either call the FastAPI endpoint OR import the recommender directly. Direct import is simpler for Streamlit Cloud.
README must include: Mermaid architecture diagram, setup instructions, API documentation, evaluation metrics, and tech stack rationale.
Code must be modular — no single file over 200 lines.
